comment: Quality Gates catalog — code dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/code/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/code/.

sources: scripts/check_clippy_lint_loaded.sh (migrated to quality/gates/code/clippy-lint-loaded.sh per SIMPLIFY-04 P58 Wave C)

asserts: clippy.toml exists at workspace root
clippy.toml lists each of: reqwest::Client::new, reqwest::Client::builder, reqwest::ClientBuilder::new under [disallowed-methods]
no source file outside crates/reposix-core/src/http.rs constructs reqwest::Client / reqwest::ClientBuilder directly
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings exits 0

rows.owner_hint: if RED: clippy.toml is missing or has been edited to drop a reqwest entry; OR a new file constructs reqwest::Client directly outside http.rs (security regression — see CLAUDE.md threat-model § 'Outbound HTTP allowlist')

asserts: the most recent ci.yml `test` job for the current branch exited 0

rows.owner_hint: if RED: ci.yml test job is failing for the current branch -- run gh run view <run-id> --log-failed. D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr and demoted to on-demand -- ci.yml's dedicated `test` job (cargo test --workspace --locked) is the canonical enforcement venue; this row's own verifier only polls `gh run list` for the most recent successful run (needs gh CLI + auth, and is circular when run from inside the very CI run it's checking). Kept on-demand for manual post-hoc audits (e.g. "did main's last CI run actually pass").

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): reason still true -- cargo nextest run --workspace still consumes 6-15 minutes, still violates the CLAUDE.md ONE-cargo-at-a-time memory-budget rule, and still exceeds the pre-pr 10-minute cadence cap on this VM. ci.yml's test job remains the canonical enforcement venue. The 'v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P97 (Good-to-haves polish + milestone close, launch-readiness slot) -- per-row local cargo enforcement alternatives (or formally retiring this row in favor of CI-only)

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the most recent ci.yml test job for the branch actually failed, `gh run list` would report a non-success conclusion for that run and the verifier would exit non-zero naming it -- a real CI failure is the falsifying evidence, not a local proxy signal.

sources: scripts/check_fixtures.py (migrated to quality/gates/code/check-fixtures.py per SIMPLIFY-05 P58 Wave C, Option A)

asserts: github_issues.json: 4-12 KB JSON array, >=3 issues, required keys present, no credential-shaped strings
confluence_pages.json: 6-16 KB JSON object with results[], >=3 pages, ADF body re-parseable, no secrets/PII
fixtures/README.md: contains required terms (offline, content_hash, github_issues.json, confluence_pages.json, synthetic)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a benchmark fixture file has been edited (added secrets, dropped required keys, drifted size). Investigate via `python3 quality/gates/code/check-fixtures.py` locally; do NOT silence.

sources: .githooks/pre-push:61 (invokes python3 quality/runners/run.py --cadence pre-push, which composes this row)

asserts: cargo fmt --all -- --check exits 0 (no formatting drift in any workspace crate)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: cargo fmt regressed; run cargo fmt --all locally and commit the result. D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr -- ci.yml's dedicated `fmt` job runs the identical command with rust-cache reuse; a second cargo fmt in quality-pre-pr would be pure duplicate compute. Row still gates pre-push locally.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If any workspace crate had formatting drift, `cargo fmt --all -- --check` would exit non-zero and print the offending diff, which the verifier greps for and reports as FAIL -- the 'no formatting drift' claim is falsified directly by the tool this row wraps, not by a proxy signal.

sources: .githooks/pre-push:61 (invokes python3 quality/runners/run.py --cadence pre-push, which composes this row)

asserts: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings exits 0

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a new clippy violation; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --fix to auto-fix where possible. D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr -- ci.yml's dedicated `clippy` job runs the identical command with rust-cache reuse; a second clippy pass in quality-pre-pr would double compile time for no new signal. Row still gates pre-push locally. Pivot rule: if cargo clippy in pre-push exceeds 60s on warm cache, P60 Wave E may move it OUTSIDE the runner; document in SURPRISES.md.

rows.comment: Stage-2 catalog-row minting (2026-07-04). Substrate already exists (commit ca7cb61): capabilities_match_create_impl tests in reposix-github, reposix-jira, reposix-confluence.
rows.command: cargo test -p reposix-github -p reposix-jira -p reposix-confluence capabilities_match

asserts: cargo test -p reposix-github -p reposix-jira -p reposix-confluence capabilities_match exits 0
at least 3 tests matched and ran (guards against a silent 0-matches false-pass if a test name or --lib filter drifts)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a connector's CAPABILITIES constant claims support for an operation (e.g. BackendFeature::Create) that create_record actually returns Error::NotSupported for, or vice versa -- check the connector's supports() impl against its create_record match arms
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs `cargo test -p reposix-github -p reposix-jira -p reposix-confluence capabilities_match` and parses the libtest summary lines to confirm >=3 tests ran and none failed. If the claim ('each connector's CAPABILITIES constant agrees with observable create_record behavior') were false -- e.g. a connector's supports(Create) returned true while create_record actually returned Err(NotSupported), or the test filter matched 0 tests because a test was renamed/deleted -- the assertion would fail because cargo test would report a FAILED test or the parsed ran-count would be 0, and the verifier would exit non-zero naming which.

rows.comment: P106 (v0.14.0 wave-2) lost-update guard. SURPRISES-INTAKE.md 2026-07-12 08:10 (HIGH, data-loss). Shared-cache last_fetched_at cursor no longer GATES push-side conflict detection: every pushed Update is version-checked against the backend (the SoT arbiter), so a stale-base push under an advanced shared cursor is REJECTED (fetch first) instead of silently clobbering a sibling clone's edit.

asserts: cargo test -p reposix-remote --lib stale_base_push_rejected_when_shared_cursor_advanced_past_concurrent_write exits 0
exactly 1 test matched and ran (guards against a silent 0-matches false-pass if the test name drifts)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: precheck_export_against_changed_set has regressed to gating the per-record version check on list_changed_since delta membership (the shared wall-clock cursor is untrustworthy under concurrent clones — see crates/reposix-remote/src/precheck.rs Step-3 lost-update-guard rationale). A Proceed outcome for a stale-base push while the backend is ahead = SILENT LOST UPDATE (data loss). Do NOT waive.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs the regression, which builds a REAL Cache (oid_map populated + last_fetched_at advanced to now by build_from) plus an AdvancedCursorMock backend holding issue 1 at version 2 with a past updated_at, so list_changed_since(now) returns an EMPTY delta. It then calls the real precheck with a stale-base (v0) push of issue 1 and asserts Conflicts([(1,0,2,_)]). If the claim ('the shared-cursor cursor no longer gates conflict detection; every pushed Update is version-checked against the backend') were false, the precheck would return Proceed (delta empty → old gate skips the check) and the assert would fail because the test panics on Proceed — the falsifying evidence is a real lost-update Proceed, not a proxy signal.

asserts: kcov is available
coverable-line counter validated within 15% of kcov on all executed scripts
aggregate shell line-coverage >= floor (quality/shell-coverage-floor.txt)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: (a) 'kcov is available' fails -> install it. On modern distros (Ubuntu 24.04 noble dropped kcov from apt) grab the prebuilt release binary (curl -sSL https://github.com/SimonKagstrom/kcov/releases/download/v42/kcov-amd64.tar.gz | sudo tar -xz -C /usr/local) or build from source (v43: apt-get install binutils-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev libdw-dev libiberty-dev zlib1g-dev libssl-dev cmake g++, then cmake build/install); on focal/20.04 sudo apt-get install -y kcov still works. The gate/CI both depend on it; on a dev box without kcov the P2 row degrades to non-blocking locally, but the CI shell-coverage job hard-fails. (b) the aggregate dropped below the ratchet floor in quality/shell-coverage-floor.txt -> a shell script lost test coverage or a new never-run script entered the corpus; add a harness under quality/gates/code/shell-coverage-tests/ to exercise it, or (only if the drop is justified) lower the floor with a note. (c) the counter-validation assert fails -> coverable_line_count in scripts/shell_coverage.py drifted >15% from kcov on an executed script; retune it. NEVER raise the floor above the measured aggregate to force-pass.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs scripts/shell_coverage.py, which drives every harness under quality/gates/code/shell-coverage-tests/ through kcov, merges the runs, and computes the aggregate line-coverage across the full in-scope shell corpus (never-run scripts counted at 0% in the denominator). If the claim ('aggregate shell coverage is at or above the floor, and the coverable-line counter tracks kcov within 15%') were false -- coverage regressed below quality/shell-coverage-floor.txt, or the Python line-counter drifted from kcov's own total on an executed script -- the driver appends the failing assertion to asserts_failed and exits 1, and the gate propagates that non-zero exit; a passing floor and a clean counter are the falsifying evidence, not a proxy signal.

rows.comment: D-CONV-4 (2026-07-12): closes the systemic hole where 8 GREEN phases shipped over a RED main because phase-close verified the push LANDED but never that CI went GREEN on main afterward. This row runs at the NEW `post-push` cadence -- AFTER `git push origin main` lands, orchestrator/verifier-side. That timing is load-bearing and NON-CIRCULAR: it is the opposite of D-CONV-1's concern, which demoted `code/cargo-*` / `ci-job-status` out of the pre-* path because a CI-green check running INSIDE (or BEFORE) the CI run for the very commit under test is circular (CI has not concluded yet). A post-push check reads the conclusion of the run that CI has ALREADY started for the just-landed commit -- there is nothing circular about asking 'did main's latest CI go green?' once the push is on main. Crucially this asserts the LATEST run on main, NOT `gh run list --status=success` (which finds the newest GREEN run even when the latest run is RED, masking a red HEAD -- see ci-job-status.sh's deliberately-different query). If the latest ci.yml run on main is red/cancelled, this row FAILs (blast_radius P0) and verdict.py --phase rolls the phase RED. Do NOT re-demote this to pre-push 'because ci-job-status was demoted' -- ci-job-status was demoted for circularity that does NOT apply to a post-push orchestrator-side read.
sources: quality/gates/code/ci-green-on-main.sh (gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --branch=main --limit=1, single latest run, no --status filter)
quality/PROTOCOL.md Step 6 (post-push runner invocation, AFTER the push)

asserts: the LATEST ci.yml run on main concluded success (not merely that some older successful run exists) -- exit 0 only when the single most-recent run's conclusion == success
a red/cancelled/failed LATEST run makes the verifier exit 1 (FAIL), grading the phase RED
gh missing/unauthenticated OR the latest run still in-progress writes NOT-VERIFIED (exit 75, per the runner's exit-75->NOT-VERIFIED convention), never skip-as-PASS

rows.owner_hint: if RED: main's LATEST CI run concluded failure/cancelled -- do NOT open a new phase over a red main; fix-forward or revert the offending commit, wait for CI to go green, then re-run `python3 quality/runners/run.py --cadence post-push --persist`. if NOT-VERIFIED: gh CLI missing/unauth (run `gh auth login`) or CI still in-progress (wait for the run to complete, then re-grade). NEVER treat NOT-VERIFIED or an in-progress run as a pass.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier calls `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --branch=main --limit=1 --json databaseId,conclusion,status` (NO --status filter) and parses the SINGLE newest run. If the claim ('main's latest CI is green') were false -- the most-recent ci.yml run on main concluded failure or was cancelled -- the parsed conclusion != 'success' and the verifier exits 1, which run.py maps to FAIL and verdict.py --phase rolls up to RED (blast_radius P0). The falsifying evidence is the conclusion of the exact run at main's HEAD, not a proxy: unlike a `--status=success` query that would surface an OLDER green run and hide a red HEAD, --limit=1 with no status filter can only report the true latest state, so a red main cannot masquerade as green. An unknowable state (gh absent/unauth, or the run still in-progress) exits 75 -> NOT-VERIFIED (also RED for a P0 row at grading), never a pass.
